Sam Harper

Game Development • Software Engineering

About Me

Education

Currently studying Computer Science & Engineering with a specialization in Computer Graphics & Game Design at The Ohio State University. I'm in my second year of school, and I'm expecting to graduate in Spring 2028. Apart from my studies, I'm also a member of The Best Damn Band in the Land where I play trombone, and help to continue the university's tradition of musical and marching excellence.

Skills

I'm proficient in C#, C, Java, JavaScript, HTML, and CSS. C# is my personal favorite of the languages I've learned. I've also taken classes using Matlab and Assembly. Some of my most used tools lately are MonoGame and Unity for game development, and Astro and React for web-based projects.

Interests

I like creating unique and engaging experiences. I enjoy finding the perfect balance between functionality and ease of use. I like to design with one primary focus at the center of everything, while still paying attention to the details. Above all else, I hope to find mentors that help me actualize my dreams of creating unique and impactful products for others.

Projects

Temporal Stasis

"Can Time be stopped?" This was the theme of the 2026 Game I/O game jam at Ohio State. Over a two-week period in the midst of classes, extracurriculars, and midterm exams, my team of four developed a 2D puzzle platformer in Unity centered around freezing time. I primarily contributed as a gameplay programmer and level designer. Click to learn more!

Play on Itch.io!

Super Mario Bros. 3 Recreation

A recreation of Super Mario Bros. 3 for the Nintendo Entertainment System. This is a group project for my Game Development class (CSE 3902), so I can't post the public GitHub link until the class ends. I'll still give development updates regardless. Coded in C# using MonoGame.

Peggle-like Physics Demo

A demo of physics similar to the video game Peggle by PopCap. I made this out of love for the source material, but I haven't yet thought of an interesting way to expand on the original game's formula. I'm hoping to come back to the project eventually with new ideas. Coded in C# using MonoGame.
